channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Police arrest woman for assaulting 12-year-old niece with knife in Abia
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Abia State Police have arrested Nwoko Gift for allegedly assaulting her 12-year-old niece with a knife. The incident, captured on video, led to her arrest.
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-arrest-woman-for-assaulting-12-year-old-niece-with-knife-in-abia/

item.title: Burial dates for late Oyo monarch set
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Olubadan Advisory Council has approved burial dates for the late Osi Balogun of Ibadanland, Oba Olubunmi Isioye-Dada. Find details of the Oyo monarch b
Read More: https://punchng.com/burial-dates-for-late-oyo-monarch-set/

item.title: ‎State police calls stem from federal police failure — Okiro
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Former IGP Mike Okiro argues that calls for state police in Nigeria stem from the federal police’s failure due to poor funding and equipment. Learn more.
Read More: https://punchng.com/state-police-calls-stem-from-federal-police-failure-okiro/

item.title: Ronaldo’s World Cup journey ends as Spain beat Portugal 1-0 to reach quarterfinals
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Cristiano Ronaldo’s World Cup journey concludes as Spain defeats Portugal 1-0 in a tense Round of 16 clash. Read about Ronaldo’s final tournament appearanc
Read More: https://punchng.com/ronaldos-world-cup-journey-ends-as-spain-beat-portugal-1-0-to-reach-quarterfinals/

item.title: Tinubu orders probe of Meta, Google, X over alleged exploitation of news content
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu has ordered the Federal Competition and Consumer Protection Commission (FCCPC) to investigate global technology companies, including Meta, Google's parent company Alphabet, and X (formerly Twitter), over allegations of anti-competitive practices and the exploitation of ne

item.title: FEPPPAN Hails Tinubu Over N39bn Pension Debt Clearance
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Federal Parastatals and Private Sector Pensioners Association of Nigeria (FEPPPAN) has commended President Bola Ahmed Tinubu for approving the liquidation of more than N39 billion in inherited Defined Benefit Scheme (DBS) pension liabilities. The pensioners’ union, in a statement jointly s

item.title: Oil Holds Drop as Saudi Arabian Price Cut Amplifies Glut Concern
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Oil held a drop on signs of growing global oversupply, with Saudi Arabia slashing prices of its main grade to Asia and maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz picking up.

item.title: Reps Pass 363 Bills In 3 Years, Introduced 2,747 Bills
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: …1782 Awaiting Second Reading The House of Representatives has passed a total of 363 bills as of June 15, 2026, marking three years of its four-year legislative tenure. The Chairman of the Committee on Rules and Business, Hon. Francis Waive (APC, Edo), disclosed this on Monday while giving a breakd

item.title: 2027: Presidency urges media influencers to guard against misinformation
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Ahead of the 2027 General Elections, the Senior Special Assistant to President Bola Ahmed Tinubu on Media and Public Enlightenment, Abdulaziz Abdulaziz, has charged media influencers to guard against misinformation in the discharge of their assignments. The post 2027: Presidency urges media influenc

item.title: I’m not worried about Obi, northerners will never vote for him – Ali Modu Sheriff
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: When reminded that Obi won substantial votes in states including Plateau and Nasarawa during the last presidential election, Sheriff maintained that circumstances had since changed. The post I’m not worried about Obi, northerners will never vote for him – Ali Modu Sheriff appeared first

item.title: Spain dump Ronaldo's Portugal out of World Cup with stoppage-time win
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Spain laboured to an uninspiring 1-0 victory over Iberian rivals Portugal in their last-16 tie to set up a quarter-final meeting with the winners of USA vs Belgium.

item.title: Japan Drives Asia Offshore Bonds to Record, Surpassing China Era
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Japanese borrowers drove quarterly issuance in Asia Pacific’s offshore bond market to a record, eclipsing the previous peak set during an era of Chinese corporate dominance.

item.title: Food Insecurity: WEP’s Report On Acute Hunger, Humanitarian Disaster – ADC
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) has described the United Nations World Food Programme (WFP) report that more than 17 million Nigerians, including infants and young children, are facing acute hunger as a growing humanitarian disaster, and said it is an indication of President Bola Tinubu‘

item.title: Police To Destroy Expired Explosives in Rivers
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Rivers State Police Command has announced the planned destruction of expired explosive materials belonging to Halliburton Energy Services Nigeria Limited, urging residents of Kporghor Community in Tai Local Government Area of the state not to panic during the exercise. The post Police To Destroy

item.title: Police nab 150 suspects, recover 100 unregistered motorcycles, firearms in Lagos
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Lagos State Police Command has arrested 150 suspects and recovered 100 unregistered motorcycles and firearms during a coordinated raid on criminal hideouts at Boolar, Okofilling Bus Stop, Igando, and other identified black spots within the Area M, Idimu axis of the state. The post Police nab 150

item.title: Kwara APC G15 Relaunches OTOGE Movement, Stages Massive Solidarity Walk in Ilorin
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Kwara APC G15 coalition on Monday staged a massive solidarity walk across Ilorin, the Kwara State capital, declaring the relaunch of the OTOGE movement that ended the dominance of the Saraki political dynasty in the state in 2019. The procession, which attracted thousands of supporters, began at

item.title: Gunmen invade Rivers community, kill two
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Gunmen invaded Rumuodogo 2 Community in Rivers State, killing two people. Police confirm the incident, with investigations ongoing into the motive.
Read More: https://punchng.com/gunmen-invade-rivers-community-kill-two-2/

item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Police arrest 10 suspects, seizing bladed weapons, military-style clothes and bomb‑making instructions.

item.title: 2027: Makinde’s APM targets insecurity, economic recovery in governance plans
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Allied Peoples Movement (APM) says its main target is to revive the country’s economy and combat spiralling insecurity in the march to be in office next year. According to the party, an elaborate blueprint is underway to address the issues and to be unveiled soon. Oyo State Governor, Seyi

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Bauchi State chapter of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) has declared its determination to win the governorship, all three senatorial seats, the 12 House of Representatives seats and the State House of Assembly in the 2027 general elections. The State Chairman of the party, Hon. Ibrahim Kassim

item.title: Spain into quarter-finals after Merino's late winner against Portugal
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Spain leave it late as super-sub Mikel Merino scores a stoppage-time winner to send his side into the quarter-finals, ending Cristiano Ronaldo's hopes of World Cup glory.

item.title: Cultists planning to mark ‘Cultist Day’, Osun police read Riot Act
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: According to police cultists are planning to mark Tuesday 7 July with nationwide celebration of cultism and initiate secondary school students massively among others into their fold.

item.title: Ultra-processed foods are addictive like tobacco, researchers warn
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Many ultra-processed foods may trigger addictive behaviours similar to tobacco products because they are deliberately engineered to stimulate the brain's reward system... The post Ultra-processed foods are addictive like tobacco, researchers warn appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Rivian Automotive Inc. is offering to sell 75 million shares as the electric vehicle company seeks to fund equity contributions related to a US Department of Energy loan.

item.title: 15 Killed, 17 Injured In Kwara Truck Crash
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: No fewer than 15 passengers lost their lives, while 17 others sustained varying degrees of injuries in a lone truck accident along the Ilorin–Jebba Expressway in Kwara State in the early hours of Monday. The Federal Road Safety Corps (FRSC) said 16 other passengers were rescued unhurt from the crash

item.title: NPFL: Abia Warriors begin pre-season training ahead new season
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Abia Warriors have commenced pre-season training for the 2026-27 season, DAILY POST reports. The Umu Chineke held their first training session at the Umuahia Township Stadium on Monday. The training session was supervised by head coach Imama Amapakabo. Abia Warriors missed out on a continental slot

item.title: Starmer intervened in row over Mexico-England kick-off time
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Fifa had been set to bring the match forward by six hours, due to a forecast of thunderstorms.

item.title: 2026 World Cup: Gary Neville predicts England vs Norway quarter-final clash
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former Manchester United captain, Gary Neville, has predicted the quarter-final clash between England and Norway at the 2026 World Cup. Neville believes the Three Lions have what it takes to send Erling Haaland co home. He however admits it would be a tough encounter. England beat co-hosts Mexico 3-

item.title: Lagos saves N118bn property, rescues 1,900 lives
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: According to him, Lagos has intensified enforcement of fire safety regulations through stricter inspections and implementation of the Fire Safety Levy, stressing that compliance must go beyond obtaining certificates to ensuring that safety equipment and systems remain fully functional.

item.title: 【今朝の5本】仕事を始める前に読んでおきたい厳選ニュース
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: 世界トップ5目指すとMUFG社長、SKハイニックス米上場へ、米株見通し、サウジ原油値下げ、ホルムズ脱出へ

item.title: World Cup 2026: Spain edge Portugal with late Merino goal to reach quarter-finals
item.dc:creator: France 24

item.title: Taiganet.com, Home of the WS4000 Simulator
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://www.taiganet.com/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48810501 Points: 4 # Comments: 0

item.title: Tinubu: We’ll Remain Committed To Judiciary’s Welfare
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Despite barrages of criticisms trailing the present administration’s prioritised attention to the judiciary, President Bola Tinubu, on Monday, said he will remain committed to the welfare of both judges and other workers in that sector. The President disclosed this in Abuja when he inaugurated the o

item.title: Merino breaks Portugal hearts, fires Spain into quarter-finals
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The midfielder, who had come on as a substitute six minutes earlier, latched onto a precise pass from Ferran Torres inside the penalty area before calmly firing a low shot into the bottom-left corner beyond goalkeeper Diogo Costa.

item.title: Ukraine warns of interceptor missile shortage as 23 killed in Kyiv region
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: President Zelensky says Sunday's "massive Russian attack" on Kyiv consisted of 68 missiles and 351 strike drones.

item.title: FRCN Targets Digital Revenue Growth, Modernises Northeast Broadcast Stations
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Federal Radio Corporation of Nigeria (FRCN) is accelerating its digital transformation strategy by expanding social media monetisation, modernising broadcast infrastructure and diversifying revenue sources across its North-East stations as traditional advertising income comes under increasing pr

item.title: Bagudu: New NIMC Act Will Strengthen Digital Identity, Boost Nat’l Planning
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: …Reaffirms FG’s commitment to full implementation Minister of Budget and Economic Planning, Senator Abubakar Bagudu, has reaffirmed the Federal Government’s commitment to the full implementation of the National Identity Management Commission (NIMC) Act 2026, describing the new legislation as a

item.title: Mbappe condemns racist remarks made by Paraguayan senator
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Kylian Mbappe has described a Paraguayan politician as "despicable" and unworthy of her position after she made several racist remarks toward the France star.

item.title: 'I thought I was done!' - Menzies bounces back with title
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Cameron Menzies claimed his first title of the year at Players Championship 23, defeating Cristo Reyes 8-5 in Monday's final.

item.title: Learning to code is still worthwhile
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://stevekrouse.com/learn-to-code Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48810439 Points: 59 # Comments: 39

item.title: Court orders forfeiture of jailed ex-minister Saleh Mamman’s Abuja, Kaduna properties
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The latest forfeiture orders add to a slew of assets linked to Mr Mamman which the court earlier ordered forfeited to the federal government following Mr Mamman's conviction on corruption and money laundering charges involving N33.8 billion. The post Court orders forfeiture of jailed ex-minister Sal

item.title: Okuokpellagbe Woos Investors, Says Okpella Ready For Growth, Development
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Okuokpellagbe of Okpella, His Royal Majesty Michael E.O. Sado, has declared that the Edo North community is ready for accelerated economic growth, inviting local and foreign investors to take advantage of its peaceful environment, expanding infrastructure and improving security to establish busi

item.title: IPI Nigeria secures release of journalist Stanley Ugabe
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Mr Ugagbe was released to the President of IPI Nigeria, Musikilu Mojeed, at approximately 7:30 p.m. on Monday, 6 July 2026. The post IPI Nigeria secures release of journalist Stanley Ugabe appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.
